strategic_direction.title: Evergreen Logic Finalization
strategic_direction.reasoning: With the March virtual event data showing high engagement, the immediate focus is moving to an Evergreen model by April 1st to ensure consistent lead flow without manual event overhead.

strategic_direction.title: Google/YouTube Transition
strategic_direction.reasoning: As Facebook costs fluctuate, moving the proven 'Script' offer to YouTube will tap into higher-intent real estate searches. This aligns with Dan's current strategy item.

strategic_direction.title: VIP Upsell Optimization
strategic_direction.reasoning: The $11 VIP upgrade is a low-friction way to offset ad spend. Once the video is recorded, we should monitor the take-rate and test price points (e.g., $11 vs $27).

suggested_agenda.notes: Review status of webinar automation and VIP deliverables page.

suggested_agenda.topic: Strategic Pivot: Google & YouTube Ads Strategy
suggested_agenda.notes: Discuss Dan's plan for diversifying traffic sources away from Meta.
Address the 4-week delay on the platform comparison and marketing calendar.
Recap Jay's video recording needs for the VIP funnel.

projected_roadmap.title: SMS Reactivation Campaign for 'Script' Leads
projected_roadmap.what: A targeted SMS blast to the database of leads who downloaded the Script but didn't attend the March event. Includes crafting 3 variations of high-intent copy and setting up the response-handling workflow in GHL.
projected_roadmap.why_now: We have a large pool of recent 'Script' leads from the successful March campaign. Reactivating them for the new April 1st Evergreen webinar is a zero-ad-cost way to jumpstart the new funnel.
projected_roadmap.effort_b3x: 3 hours to draft copy and build the automation sequence in GoHighLevel.
projected_roadmap.effort_client: Final approval of SMS copy and ensuring the team is ready to handle incoming replies.
projected_roadmap.prerequisites: Evergreen Webinar automation must be live (due April 1st).
projected_roadmap.impact: Expect an immediate surge in webinar registrations and potential VIP upgrades without additional Meta ad spend.
projected_roadmap.pitch_script: Jay, we've got a goldmine of leads from the March campaign who didn't quite make it to the finish line. I want to run a quick SMS reactivation sequence to pull them into our new evergreen webinar. It’s a fast way to get the new system 'fed' with zero extra ad spend—does that sound like a win to you?

projected_roadmap.title: YouTube Ad Creative 'Hook' Suite
projected_roadmap.what: Development of 5 specific video scripts designed for YouTube's skip-friendly format. This includes 'The Credibility Hook,' 'The Result Hook,' and 'The Myth-Buster Hook' specifically for the Private Money Authority brand.
projected_roadmap.why_now: Dan is already mapping out the Google/YouTube transition. The biggest bottleneck in moving from Meta to YouTube is creative—we need specifically structured scripts to succeed on that platform.
projected_roadmap.effort_b3x: 4 hours for scriptwriting and providing a 'how-to' filming guide for Jay.
projected_roadmap.effort_client: Jay needs to film the 5 hooks (approx. 45 minutes of total filming time).
projected_roadmap.prerequisites: Strategy for Google and YouTube Ads transition (Dan Kuschell).
projected_roadmap.impact: Lowering CPL (Cost Per Lead) by moving to high-intent video traffic and building long-term platform stability.
projected_roadmap.pitch_script: As Dan and I look at moving us onto YouTube, the key to winning there is having the right 'hooks' in the first 5 seconds of your videos. I’ve mapped out a suite of 5 scripts for you to film that will make this transition much smoother. If you can commit 45 minutes to recording these, we can get that YouTube traffic flowing.

projected_roadmap.title: Post-Webinar 'Downsell' Email Sequence
projected_roadmap.what: A 4-part email sequence for webinar attendees who didn't purchase the main offer, offering a lower-tier product or a high-value lead magnet to keep them in the ecosystem.
projected_roadmap.why_now: With the transition to an Evergreen model, we need to maximize the ROI of every attendee. Currently, if they don't buy, the journey stalls. This fills a significant 'leaky bucket' in the current funnel.
projected_roadmap.effort_b3x: 5 hours for copywriting and technical implementation in the email platform.
projected_roadmap.effort_client: Confirm which 'downsell' offer (e.g., a specific book or smaller course) we should lead with.
projected_roadmap.impact: Increases total funnel revenue by capturing leads who are interested but not yet ready for the high-ticket commitment.
projected_roadmap.pitch_script: Now that the evergreen machine is running, I want to make sure we aren't leaving money on the table for people who aren't quite ready for the full program. I'd like to build out a 'downsell' sequence that keeps them engaged with a smaller offer. It’s about making sure that even if they say 'not now' to the big offer, they still say 'yes' to staying in Jay's world.

talking_points.Convert PDF offer document into PowerPoi: Great news, Jay—Ashley finished converting that PDF offer into slides, so you’re all set for the upcoming presentations.
talking_points.Launch Facebook Ads for March 21st Virtu: We saw strong momentum from the March 21st ad run, which really proved the 'Script' offer works as a lead magnet.
talking_points.Introduce Darrin and Jay to Ron for sale: Glad Dan was able to get that intro to Ron handled; getting a star closer in place is going to be huge as the evergreen leads start scaling.
talking_points.Configure Once-a-Day Evergreen Webinar a: Phil's almost finished with the evergreen logic—we're on track to have that automation live by tomorrow, April 1st.
talking_points.Record Webinar VIP Bundle script video: Jay, we still need that VIP Bundle script video from your side—it’s the last piece we need to really maximize the revenue from this new evergreen funnel.
talking_points.Send email platform comparison to Jay: I noticed we haven't touched the email platform comparison in a few weeks. Tyler, can we get that over to Jay by Friday so he can make a call on the tech stack?
talking_points.Populate Marketing Calendar for 3 months: Ashley, let's make sure we get that 3-month calendar populated this week so Jay’s team knows exactly what’s coming down the pipe.
talking_points.Scale Script Facebook Campaign budget: Jay, the data on the Script campaign still looks great. Should we go ahead and pull the trigger on scaling that budget today?
talking_points.SMS Reactivation Campaign for 'Script' L: Jay, we have a ton of leads from the script download who haven't bought yet. I want to run a quick SMS reactivation to pull them into the new webinar—it’s a fast, free win for us.
talking_points.Strategy for Google and YouTube Ads tran: Dan’s mapping out our move to YouTube traffic now; it’s the right time to start diversifying so we aren't just relying on Facebook's algorithm.
talking_points.Consult Tyler regarding GoHighLevel text: Dan, did you get a chance to sync with Tyler on the GHL text throttling? We want to make sure the voice broadcasts don't get blocked.
